curriculum.subject: Accountancy
curriculum.overallDescription: 8 units, 8 chapters, and 5 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify / recognise the individual(s) and entities that use accounting information for serving their needs of decision making.
Explain the various terms used in accounting and differentiate between different related terms like current and non-current, capital and revenue.
Give examples of terms like business transaction, liabilities, assets, expenditure and purchases.
Explain that sales/purchases include both cash and credit sales/purchases relating to the accounting year.
Differentiate among income, profits and gains.
State the meaning of fundamental accounting assumptions and their relevance in accounting.
Describe the meaning of accounting assumptions and the situation in which an assumption is applied during the accounting process.
Explain the meaning, applicability, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting standards.
Appreciate that various accounting standards developed nationally and globally are in practice for bringing parity in the accounting treatment of different items.
Acknowledge the fact that recording of accounting transactions follows double entry system.
Explain the bases of recording accounting transaction and to appreciate that accrual basis is a better basis for depicting the correct financial position of an enterprise.

topics: Introduction to Accounting and Qualitative Characteristics
Basic Accounting Terms
Recording of Business Transactions and Books of Original Entry
Introduction to Accounting and Theoretical Framework
Concept, Objectives, Limitations of Accounting
Qualitative Characteristics and Role in Business
Basic Accounting Terms: Entity, Assets, Liabilities, Capital
Fundamental Assumptions (GAAP): Going Concern, Money Measurement, etc.
Accounting Standards and GST Overview
Accounting Process and Recording Transactions
Source Documents, Vouchers, and Accounting Equation
Books of Original Entry: Journal, Cash Book (Simple & Bank)
Special Purpose Books: Purchase/Sales Day Books and Returns
Ledger Posting, Balancing Accounts, and Trial Balance
Depreciation Methods (SLM/WDV) and Provisions

subtopics: Source Documents, Vouchers, and Accounting Equation

practicals.title: Practical Work: Computerised Accounting System
practicals.details: Steps in installation of CAS, codification and Hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts. Data Entry, validation and verification. Adjusting entries, preparation of balance sheet, profit and loss account with closing entries and opening entries.

projects.title: Project Work: Collection of source documents and recording transactions
projects.details: Collection of source documents, preparation of vouchers, recording of transactions with the help of vouchers.

projects.title: Project Work: Bank Reconciliation Statement
projects.details: Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement with the given cash book and the pass book with twenty to twenty-five transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Comprehensive Sole Proprietorship Business Project
projects.details: Comprehensive project of any sole proprietorship business. This may start with journal entries and their ledgering, preparation of Trial balance, Trading and Profit and Loss Account and Balance Sheet. Expenses, incomes and profit (loss), assets and liabilities are to be depicted using pie chart / bar diagram. This may include simple GST related transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Financial Statement Analysis
projects.details: One specific project based on financial statement analysis of a company covering any two aspects from the following: 1. Comparative and common size financial statements, 2. Accounting Ratios, 3. Segment Reports, 4. Cash Flow Statements.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (80 Marks), Project Work (20 Marks)
Class XII Part A & B: Theory (60 Marks for Partnership/Companies, 12 Marks for Analysis, 8 Marks for Cash Flow) or Computerized Accounting (20 Marks); Practical/Project Work (20 Marks)

question_paper_design.typology_1_remembering_understanding: 32 Marks (40%) - Recall facts, terms, concepts; organize and interpret information.
question_paper_design.typology_2_applying: 24 Marks (30%) - Solve problems in new situations using acquired knowledge.
question_paper_design.typology_3_analyzing_evaluating_creating: 24 Marks (30%) - Examine parts, make judgments based on criteria, propose alternative solutions.

question_paper_design.typology_1_remembering_understanding: 32 Marks (40%) - Recall facts, terms, concepts; organize and interpret information.

question_paper_design.typology_3_analyzing_evaluating_creating: 24 Marks (30%) - Examine parts, make judgments based on criteria, propose alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Class XI: Focus on basic concepts, process of accounting, GST calculations, and ICT introduction.
Class XII Part A: Partnership Firms (36 Marks), Companies Accounts (24 Marks).
Class XII Part B: Financial Statement Analysis (12+8=20 Marks) OR Computerized Accounting (20 Marks).

practical_assessment.class_xii_part_b_option_1: Practical Work (20 Marks) including Practical File (12 Marks) and Viva Voce (8 Marks).
practical_assessment.description: Includes installation of CAS, codification, data entry/validation, adjusting entries, balance sheet preparation.

project_assessment.class_xi_project_work: Project Work (20 Marks): Collection of source documents, voucher preparation, recording transactions, Bank Reconciliation Statement, or Comprehensive sole proprietorship project with visualizations.
project_assessment.class_xii_part_a_project_work: Project File (12 Marks) and Viva Voce (8 Marks). Specific project on financial statement analysis covering two aspects: Comparative/Common size statements, Accounting Ratios, Segment Reports, or Cash Flow Statements.
project_assessment.guidelines: Students select a business form, visit for realistic data, prepare trial balance, P&L account, Balance Sheet with adjustments.

competency_groups.title: Theoretical Framework Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to accounting concepts, assumptions, and GST for Class XI.
competencies: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify / recognise the individual(s) and entities that use accounting information for serving their needs of decision making.
Explain the various terms used in accounting and differentiate between different related terms like current and non-current, capital and revenue.
Give examples of terms like business transaction, liabilities, assets, expenditure and purchases.
Explain that sales/purchases include both cash and credit sales/purchases relating to the accounting year.
Differentiate among income, profits and gains.
State the meaning of fundamental accounting assumptions and their relevance in accounting.
Describe the meaning of accounting assumptions and the situation in which an assumption is applied during the accounting process.
Explain the meaning, applicability, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting standards.
Appreciate that various accounting standards developed nationally and globally are in practice for bringing parity in the accounting treatment of different items.
Acknowledge the fact that recording of accounting transactions follows double entry system.
Explain the bases of recording accounting transaction and to appreciate that accrual basis is a better basis for depicting the correct financial position of an enterprise.
Explain the meaning, advantages and characteristic of GST.

competency_groups.title: Accounting Process Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to recording transactions, books of accounts, and adjustments for Class XI.
competencies: Explain the concept of accounting equation and appreciate that every transaction affects either both the sides of the equation or a positive effect on one item and a negative effect on another item on the same side of accounting equation.
Explain the effect of a transaction (increase or decrease) on the assets, liabilities, capital, revenue and expenses.
Develop an understanding that documents, accounting vouchers are prepared for recording transactions in the books of accounts.
Develop the skill of calculating GST.
Explain the purpose of maintaining a Cash Book and develop the skill of preparing the format of different types of cash books and the method of recording cash transactions in Cash book.
Describe the method of recording transactions other than cash transactions as per their nature in different subsidiary books.

Appreciate that at times bank balance as indicated by cash book is different from the bank balance as shown by the pass book / bank statement and to reconcile both the balances, bank reconciliation statement is prepared.
Develop understanding of preparing bank reconciliation statement.
Understand the necessity of providing depreciation and develop the skill of using different methods for computing depreciation.

Appreciate that transactions are posted from subsidiary books and journal proper into the concerned accounts in the ledger to ascertain the position of individual accounts, and develop the skill of ledger posting.
Develop understanding of preparing bank reconciliation statement.
Understand the accounting treatment of providing depreciation directly to the concerned asset account or by creating provision for depreciation account.
Appreciate the method of asset disposal through the concerned asset account or by preparing asset disposal account.
Appreciate the need for creating reserves and also making provisions for events which may belong to the current year but may happen in next year.
State the need and objectives of preparing trial balance and develop the skill of preparing trial balance.
Understand that errors may be committed during the process of accounting, appreciate different types of errors and their effect on trial balance.
Develop the skill of identification and location of errors and their rectification and preparation of suspense account.

competency_groups.title: Financial Statements Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to preparation of financial statements and incomplete records for Class XI.
competencies: State the meaning of financial statements and purpose of preparing them.
Develop skill of preparing trading and profit and loss account, including understanding gross profit, operating profit and net profit.
Explain the need for preparing balance sheet.
Understand the technique of grouping and marshalling of assets and liabilities.
Appreciate that there may be certain items other than those shown in trial balance which may need adjustments while preparing financial statements.
Develop understanding and skill to do adjustments for items like depreciation, closing stock, provisions, abnormal loss etc. and their presentation in financial statements.
Ascertain Profit/Loss by Statement of Affairs method (excluding conversion method) for incomplete records.

competency_groups.title: Partnership Firms Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to accounting for partnership firms, reconstitution, and dissolution.
competencies: State the meaning of partnership, partnership firm and partnership deed.
Describe the characteristic features of partnership and the contents of partnership deed.
Discuss the significance of provision of Partnership Act in absence of partnership deed.
Differentiate between fixed and fluctuating capital, outline process and develop understanding/skill for preparation of Profit and Loss Appropriation Account (including guarantee of profits).
Develop skill of making past adjustments regarding interest on capital, drawings, salary and profit sharing ratio.
State meaning, nature and factors affecting goodwill; develop skill of valuation using average profit, super profit and capitalization methods.
Understand sacrificing ratio, gaining ratio and change in profit sharing ratio among existing partners.

Develop understanding/skill for accounting treatment of revaluation assets, reassessment of liabilities, reserves, accumulated profits/losses via Revaluation Account and Balance Sheet upon admission/change in P/S ratio.
Explain effect of admission of a new partner on capital accounts; develop skill of adjustment of capital accounts and preparation of Capital/Current Accounts and Balance Sheet for the new firm.

Develop understanding/skill regarding retirement/death: treatment of goodwill, revaluation, reserves, accumulated profits/losses, adjustments to capital accounts, calculation of deceased partner's share till death, and preparation of executor's account.

Discuss situations under which a partnership firm can be dissolved; develop skill for dissolution accounting including Realisation Account, Capital Accounts, Cash/Bank A/c (excluding piecemeal distribution/sale to company/insolvency).

competency_groups.title: Company Accounts Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to accounting for share capital, debentures, and financial statements of companies.
competencies: Differentiate between equity shares and preference shares and different types of share capital.

Develop understanding/skill regarding issue/allotment of equity/preference shares (public subscription over/under-subscription, par/premium calls in advance/arrears), private placement, ESOP, Sweat Equity.
Understand accounting treatment of forfeiture and re-issue of forfeited shares; describe presentation of share capital in Balance Sheet as per Schedule III Part I of Companies Act 2013.

Explain meaning/types of debentures; develop skill regarding issue at par/premium/discount, consideration other than cash, redemption terms, collateral security concept, interest calculation (TDS excluded), and writing off discount/loss on issue as per AS-16.
Develop understanding/skill for preparation of Statement of Profit and Loss and Balance Sheet with major headings/sub-headings as per Schedule III to Companies Act 2013.

competency_groups.title: Financial Statement Analysis Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to analysis tools, ratios, and cash flow statements.
competencies: State meaning/objectives/limitations of financial statement analysis; discuss different tools (comparative/common size/ratio/cash flow).
Develop skill for preparation/computation of comparative and common size statements.
Understand computation of Liquidity Ratios: Current ratio, Quick ratio.
Develop skill for Solvency Ratios: Debt to Equity Ratio, Total Asset to Debt Ratio, Proprietary Ratio, Interest Coverage Ratio, Debt to Capital Employed Ratio.
Develop skill for Activity Ratios: Inventory Turnover, Trade Receivables/Payables Turnover, Fixed/Net Asset Turnover, Working Capital Turnover.
Develop skill for Profitability Ratios: Gross Profit Ratio, Operating Ratio, Net Profit Ratio (before and after tax), Return on Investment.

State meaning/objectives of Cash Flow Statement; develop understanding/skill for preparation using Indirect Method as per AS-3 with adjustments (depreciation/amortization, profit/loss on sale, dividends, tax).

competency_groups.title: Computerised Accounting Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to computerized accounting systems and electronic spreadsheets.
competencies: Understand introduction, features, structure of Computerised Accounting System (CAS) and software packages.
Develop understanding/skill for application in generating accounting information using Electronic Spreadsheets (BRS, asset accounting, loan schedules, ratio analysis).
Interpret data representation via graphs, charts, diagrams generated by spreadsheets.
Understand steps in installation of CAS: codification and hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts.
Develop skill for Data Entry, validation, verification; Adjusting entries; preparation of Balance Sheet/Profit & Loss Account with closing/opening entries.
Appreciate need and security features of the system.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (80 Marks), Project Work (20 Marks)
Class XII Part A & B: Theory (60 Marks for Partnership/Companies, 12 Marks for Analysis, 8 Marks for Cash Flow) or Computerized Accounting (20 Marks); Practical/Project Work (20 Marks)

Explain the meaning, advantages and characteristic of GST.

Explain the concept of accounting equation and appreciate that every transaction affects either both the sides of the equation or a positive effect on one item and a negative effect on another item on the same side of accounting equation.
Explain the effect of a transaction (increase or decrease) on the assets, liabilities, capital, revenue and expenses.
Develop an understanding that documents, accounting vouchers are prepared for recording transactions in the books of accounts.
Develop the skill of calculating GST.
Explain the purpose of maintaining a Cash Book and develop the skill of preparing the format of different types of cash books and the method of recording cash transactions in Cash book.
Describe the method of recording transactions other than cash transactions as per their nature in different subsidiary books.

Appreciate that at times bank balance as indicated by cash book is different from the bank balance as shown by the pass book / bank statement and to reconcile both the balances, bank reconciliation statement is prepared.
Develop understanding of preparing bank reconciliation statement.
Understand the necessity of providing depreciation and develop the skill of using different methods for computing depreciation.
Understand the accounting treatment of providing depreciation directly to the concerned asset account or by creating provision for depreciation account.
Appreciate the method of asset disposal through the concerned asset account or by preparing asset disposal account.
Appreciate the need for creating reserves and also making provisions for events which may belong to the current year but may happen in next year.

Appreciate that transactions are posted from subsidiary books and journal proper into the concerned accounts in the ledger to ascertain the position of individual accounts, and develop the skill of ledger posting.
State the need and objectives of preparing trial balance and develop the skill of preparing trial balance.
Understand that errors may be committed during the process of accounting, appreciate different types of errors and their effect on trial balance.
Develop the skill of identification and location of errors and their rectification and preparation of suspense account.

learning_outcomes.topic: Meaning and Purpose of Financial Statements
outcomes: State the meaning of financial statements and purpose of preparing them.

learning_outcomes.topic: Trading, Profit and Loss Account Preparation
outcomes: Develop skill of preparing trading and profit and loss account, including understanding gross profit, operating profit and net profit.
Explain the need for preparing balance sheet.
Understand the technique of grouping and marshalling of assets and liabilities.
Appreciate that there may be certain items other than those shown in trial balance which may need adjustments while preparing financial statements.
Develop understanding and skill to do adjustments for items like depreciation, closing stock, provisions, abnormal loss etc. and their presentation in financial statements.
Ascertain Profit/Loss by Statement of Affairs method (excluding conversion method) for incomplete records.
State the meaning of partnership, partnership firm and partnership deed.
Describe the characteristic features of partnership and the contents of partnership deed.
Discuss the significance of provision of Partnership Act in absence of partnership deed.
Differentiate between fixed and fluctuating capital, outline process and develop understanding/skill for preparation of Profit and Loss Appropriation Account (including guarantee of profits).
Develop skill of making past adjustments regarding interest on capital, drawings, salary and profit sharing ratio.
State meaning, nature and factors affecting goodwill; develop skill of valuation using average profit, super profit and capitalization methods.
Understand sacrificing ratio, gaining ratio and change in profit sharing ratio among existing partners.

Develop understanding/skill for accounting treatment of revaluation assets, reassessment of liabilities, reserves, accumulated profits/losses via Revaluation Account and Balance Sheet upon admission/change in P/S ratio.
Explain effect of admission of a new partner on capital accounts; develop skill of adjustment of capital accounts and preparation of Capital/Current Accounts and Balance Sheet for the new firm.

learning_outcomes.topic: Retirement or Death of a Partner
outcomes: Develop understanding/skill regarding retirement/death: treatment of goodwill, revaluation, reserves, accumulated profits/losses, adjustments to capital accounts, calculation of deceased partner's share till death, and preparation of executor's account.

Discuss situations under which a partnership firm can be dissolved; develop skill for dissolution accounting including Realisation Account, Capital Accounts, Cash/Bank A/c (excluding piecemeal distribution/sale to company/insolvency).

learning_outcomes.topic: Share Capital Types and Issue of Shares
outcomes: Differentiate between equity shares and preference shares and different types of share capital.

Develop understanding/skill regarding issue/allotment of equity/preference shares (public subscription over/under-subscription, par/premium calls in advance/arrears), private placement, ESOP, Sweat Equity.
Understand accounting treatment of forfeiture and re-issue of forfeited shares; describe presentation of share capital in Balance Sheet as per Schedule III Part I of Companies Act 2013.

Explain meaning/types of debentures; develop skill regarding issue at par/premium/discount, consideration other than cash, redemption terms, collateral security concept, interest calculation (TDS excluded), and writing off discount/loss on issue as per AS-16.
Develop understanding/skill for preparation of Statement of Profit and Loss and Balance Sheet with major headings/sub-headings as per Schedule III to Companies Act 2013.
State meaning/objectives/limitations of financial statement analysis; discuss different tools (comparative/common size/ratio/cash flow).
Develop skill for preparation/computation of comparative and common size statements.
Understand computation of Liquidity Ratios: Current ratio, Quick ratio.
Develop skill for Solvency Ratios: Debt to Equity Ratio, Total Asset to Debt Ratio, Proprietary Ratio, Interest Coverage Ratio, Debt to Capital Employed Ratio.
Develop skill for Activity Ratios: Inventory Turnover, Trade Receivables/Payables Turnover, Fixed/Net Asset Turnover, Working Capital Turnover.
Develop skill for Profitability Ratios: Gross Profit Ratio, Operating Ratio, Net Profit Ratio (before and after tax), Return on Investment.

State meaning/objectives of Cash Flow Statement; develop understanding/skill for preparation using Indirect Method as per AS-3 with adjustments (depreciation/amortization, profit/loss on sale, dividends, tax).
Understand introduction, features, structure of Computerised Accounting System (CAS) and software packages.
Develop understanding/skill for application in generating accounting information using Electronic Spreadsheets (BRS, asset accounting, loan schedules, ratio analysis).
Interpret data representation via graphs, charts, diagrams generated by spreadsheets.
Understand steps in installation of CAS: codification and hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts.
Develop skill for Data Entry, validation, verification; Adjusting entries; preparation of Balance Sheet/Profit & Loss Account with closing/opening entries.
Appreciate need and security features of the system.

projects.title: Project Work: Collection of source documents and recording transactions
projects.details: Collection of source documents, preparation of vouchers, recording of transactions with the help of vouchers.

projects.title: Project Work: Bank Reconciliation Statement
projects.details: Preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement with the given cash book and the pass book with twenty to twenty-five transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Comprehensive Sole Proprietorship Business Project
projects.details: Comprehensive project of any sole proprietorship business. This may start with journal entries and their ledgering, preparation of Trial balance, Trading and Profit and Loss Account and Balance Sheet. Expenses, incomes and profit (loss), assets and liabilities are to be depicted using pie chart / bar diagram. This may include simple GST related transactions.

projects.title: Project Work: Financial Statement Analysis
projects.details: One specific project based on financial statement analysis of a company covering any two aspects from the following: 1. Comparative and common size financial statements, 2. Accounting Ratios, 3. Segment Reports, 4. Cash Flow Statements.

practicals.title: Practical Work: Computerised Accounting System
practicals.details: Steps in installation of CAS, codification and Hierarchy of account heads, creation of accounts. Data Entry, validation and verification. Adjusting entries, preparation of balance sheet, profit and loss account with closing entries and opening entries.

dependency_graph.topic: Meaning and Purpose of Financial Statements
dependent_topics: Trading, Profit and Loss Account Preparation

dependency_graph.topic: Trading, Profit and Loss Account Preparation
prerequisites: Meaning and Purpose of Financial Statements

dependency_graph.topic: Retirement or Death of a Partner
Share Capital Types and Issue of Shares

prerequisites: Share Capital Types and Issue of Shares
Meaning and Purpose of Financial Statements

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Theory Questions on definitions and assumptions
Preparation of P&L and Balance Sheet with adjustments
Case studies on admission, retirement, and dissolution
Journal entries for share issues, forfeiture, re-issue
Computation of ratios and cash flow statements

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Very High (Software installation and data entry)
